vulnerability.reason: reference describes low-entropy text inherently degrading detection; generated frames vulnerability as reliance on nonadaptive-attack tuning, though low-entropy context is mentioned.
vulnerability.reference: Low-entropy text provides minimal embedding space, degrading detection reliability.
vulnerability.generated: Regulators depend on watermark detection tuned only against nonadaptive attacks, leaving it ineffective against adaptive evasion in low-entropy legal texts.

impact.reason: affected party (judicial system) is vaguely retained as 'institutional trust' but damage type shifts from undermining judicial integrity to eroding trust in document authenticity.
impact.reference: Undetected AI-generated legal briefs undermine judicial integrity and fairness.
impact.generated: Undetected AI-generated legal briefs circumvent oversight, enabling misuse and eroding institutional trust in document authenticity.

dose_response.reason: generated arc introduces adaptive black-box removal and reliance on nonadaptive tuning, altering the causal mechanism from reference's low-entropy detection degradation enabling evasion.
dose_response.reference: Watermark detection degradation on low-entropy text enables evasion when regulators verify legal briefs, leading to undetected AI submissions that undermine judicial integrity.
dose_response.generated: LLM watermarking susceptible to adaptive black-box removal attacks, when regulators verify AI authorship of low-entropy legal briefs, triggers reliance on detection tuned only against nonadaptive attacks, leading to undetected AI briefs circumventing oversight, enabling misuse and eroding institutional trust.
